transcript: Korea Republic and Malaysia face each other in the Aljanub Stadium on the final day of action in Group E. Son Heung-min and company still need at least a point to guarantee a top-two finish and secure automatic qualification for the knockout stages. After their draw against Jordan on Matchday 2, while Malaysia will be going into their final game playing for pride as their tournament is as good as over after failing to put any points on the board so far. Jürgen Klinsmann makes four changes to his starting 11, with Jongwoo Young and Lee Kang-in coming into midfield. Kim Tae-hwan and Kim Young-gwang come into the back four. Three changes for Malaysia, with Daniel Ting replacing captain Matthew Davies. Brendan Gann comes into the fray in midfield, and Darren Locke leads the line in attack. Son Heung-min coming through the middle here. Solo run! Great shot. And his weaker left foot as well. But an even better save by Sian Hazmi. Well, the pressure is relentless here from Korea Republic. Hanging on the front door the opening goal what a save by Hazmi or did it cross the line well VAR has decided it will be a goal Jung Woo Young with the header Hazmi couldn't keep it out Korea coming forward But again, absolutely dominating possession in this first half nice little dink inside has me called into action once more Malaysia keeper keeping Malaysia in this game Korea Republic can lead by a goal to nil that's half time in the Aljanouf Stadium Darren Locke winning the ball back in a promising position here still work to do but it's a chance for Malaysia lovely turn lovely finish how about that from Faisal Halim I'm not sure he meant that first touch, but then picks his spot and finds the gap to put Malaysia back on level terms here. And all of a sudden, Malaysia have found a little bit of rhythm to ball into the middle. That looked like a tackle on the player rather than a clearance what ifif Aiman caught by Sio Yong Wu. VAR stepping in and Malaysia have a penalty. Arif Aiman with a chance to give Malaysia the lead and he slots it into the bottom right corner. Beautifully taken penalty and what a turnaround here. Korea Republic running out of time, desperately need an equaliser, and they've found it. Lee Kang-in with a beautiful free kick, Hazmi nearly keeps it out, unfortunate for the Indonesia goalkeeper. Might we yet have a winner? All inside He has gone down And he's caught the player Rather than the ball for the third time VAR steps in it's another penalty Son Heung-min with a chance to give Korea Republic the lead in injury time and that is how you take a penalty. All that experience coming into play. Absolutely no stopping that one. Fourteen minutes of injury time here. Is there one last twist or turn? Ball comes inside. There's a shot and Malaysia have the equaliser. Can you believe it? Romo Morales with his first goal for his country he's only been on the pitch for two minutes what a time to come up with it so Korea Republic go through to the knockout stages despite dropping points again after a roller coaster of a game from start to finish in the Aljanou Stadium while Malaysia can bid farewell to the competition with their heads held high and pride restored after going toe-to-toe with one of the favourites to lift the Asian Cup trophy.
